summary: Iteration 313 — Backend-only verification of the crates→bottles bug fix in the distributor Stock Dashboard. Wrote /app/backend/tests/test_stock_dashboard_crate_bug.py (5 tests, all PASS). Confirmed GET /api/distributors/bb12d90e-4d33-4890-ac5f-17573c551b5c/stock-dashboard returns stock_delivered=36 for SKU 51cbec53-a1d5-4e23-b708-a1fd0c014059 ('20L Premium') — matches the expected 3 crates × 12 bottles/crate. Pre-fix value was 3.
action_items: Main agent: clean up the seeded delivery TEST-DELIVERY-CRATE-BUG (delivery_number TEST-DC-BUG) on distributor Brian after verification, per the review request. Note: the seeded SKU '20L Premium' currently shows stock_available=-36 (received=0, delivered=36) which is expected for a synthetic seed with no corresponding in-flow, but should not leak into production tenants.
critical_code_review_comments: The `_item_crates` helper in /app/backend/routes/distributors.py (~line 8811) now correctly returns packages * packaging_units when both are meaningful (packages>0, packaging_units>1). This is a two-way guard — it fixes both under-counts (3 stored as CRATES with pkg=3, units=12 → 36) and over-counts (10-crate x 15-bpc line → 150, not 2250). Consider adding a code comment and a unit test at the helper level (not just endpoint-level) to lock in the invariant.
distributors.py is a very large file (helper at line ~8811). Splitting stock-dashboard aggregation logic into a dedicated module under routes/stock/ would improve testability.
test_credentials: CEO surya.yadavalli@nylaairwater.earth / test123 (Tenant nyla-air-water) — POST /api/auth/login returns session_token.
seed_data_creation: None created by testing agent. Used the pre-seeded TEST-DELIVERY-CRATE-BUG delivery (main agent must clean up).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Verified endpoint response for Brian (bb12d90e-4d33-4890-ac5f-17573c551b5c): SKUs → Nyla-600ml/Silver 1857/64/1791, Nyla-330ml/Silver 166/5/146, Nyla-660ml/Sparkling 83/24/59, Nyla-660ml/Gold 24/0/24, 20L Premium 0/36/-36. Totals → received=2130, delivered=129, pending_out=17, on_hand=2001, available=1984. No SKU shows the multiplicative over-count regression (delivered ≤ received × 20 guard passes). stock_delivered=36 for the target SKU is a clean multiple of packaging_units=12, confirming base-bottle aggregation. Test module: /app/backend/tests/test_stock_dashboard_crate_bug.py — reusable for regression on future distributors.py edits.
rca of the issue: Root cause was in _item_crates helper: when a delivery line item was mis-stored with unit=CRATES but quantity=3 (crate count instead of base bottles), the helper returned quantity as-is → 3 bottles reported. Fix: when packages>0 AND packaging_units>1, authoritatively compute base bottles = packages × packaging_units (=36). This also protects against the inverse regression (quantity already in base bottles being multiplied by packaging_units). Verified live via /api/distributors/{id}/stock-dashboard on preview.
